vocab.Gli amici: The friends; My friends (context: often 'gli amici' means 'my friends')
vocab.mi hanno dato: gave me; have given me (context: 'mi' = to me, 'hanno dato' = have given/gave)

advancedVocab.mi hanno dato come: they gave me as (can mean 'they nicknamed me' or 'they called me' in context)

beginnerVocab.cucina: kitchen; cooking (note: 'cucina' can mean both 'kitchen' and 'cooking')

advancedVocab.classica: classic; traditional (can imply 'typical' or 'standard' in context)
advancedVocab.camicia/giacca: shirt/jacket (hybrid garment; not a standard term)
advancedVocab.legato a: linked to; tied to (can mean 'connected with' in a figurative sense)

advancedVocab.della tradizione: from tradition; traditional (refers to traditional cuisine)
advancedVocab.cambiando delle forme: changing the forms; altering the shapes (nuanced: refers to modifying traditional presentations or preparations)

advancedVocab.prendo due culture: I take two cultures; I bring together two cultures (note: 'prendere' here means 'to take' or 'to bring together', not just 'to take physically')
advancedVocab.il riso, che è della cucina milanese: rice, which belongs to Milanese cuisine (note: 'della cucina milanese' means 'of Milanese cuisine', indicating origin or association)

vocab.pasta alla mollica: pasta with breadcrumbs (note: 'mollica' means 'breadcrumbs', a Sicilian specialty)

advancedVocab.mollica: breadcrumbs (note: refers to the soft part of bread, often used as a topping in Sicilian cuisine)

vocab.farò: I will make; I will do

advancedVocab.mollica: breadcrumbs; soft part of bread (used in southern Italian cooking)

advancedVocab.ci servono: we need; are needed by us (note: 'ci' as indirect object pronoun, common in recipes)
advancedVocab.allora: so; well then (discourse marker, often used to transition or emphasize)

advancedVocab.adoro: I love; I adore (stronger than 'like', often used for emphasis)
advancedVocab.riso carnaroli: Carnaroli rice (a specific Italian rice variety, prized for risotto)
advancedVocab.consistenza: texture; consistency (in cooking, refers to mouthfeel or firmness)
advancedVocab.cottura: cooking; doneness (refers to the process or degree of being cooked)

vocab.mantecare: to cream; to stir in (cooking term, especially for risotto)

beginnerVocab.maggiore: greater; bigger; older (note: can mean 'older' for people, 'greater' or 'bigger' for things)

advancedVocab.fa fatica a: struggles to; has difficulty (idiomatic, means 'is hard to')
advancedVocab.mantecare: cream; stir in (culinary term, especially for risotto)

advancedVocab.rompere la parte basica: break the basic/alkaline part (neutralize basic flavors; cooking chemistry)

vocab.la famosa onda: the famous wave (note: refers to the creamy texture in risotto)

advancedVocab.onda (la famosa onda): wave (the famous wave; refers to the creamy, wavelike texture of risotto)

vocab.non c'è: is not there; is not present

advancedVocab.che non c'è: that isn't there; that's missing (note: idiomatic for 'not included')
advancedVocab.lo aggiungo io: I add it myself (note: emphasis on personal action/choice)

advancedVocab.Facciamo arrabbiare tutti: Let's make everyone angry; Let's annoy everyone (ironic/humorous expression)
advancedVocab.teglia da forno: baking tray; oven pan (specific kitchenware term)

advancedVocab.assistente: assistant (can refer to a helper, aide, or subordinate, not always a formal job title)

vocab.simpatico: nice; pleasant (about a person or thing)

advancedVocab.piacere: pleasure; nice to meet you (used as a greeting or introduction)
advancedVocab.simpatico: nice; pleasant (can refer to appearance or personality, not just 'funny')
advancedVocab.magari: maybe; perhaps; if only (expresses hope or suggestion)

advancedVocab.ti copre: covers you; hides you (note: 'coprire' can mean to cover physically or to conceal)
advancedVocab.Mettiamo a esiccare: let's put to dry (note: 'esiccare' is a less common variant of 'essiccare', meaning to dry or dehydrate)
advancedVocab.acciuga: anchovy (note: a common ingredient in Italian cuisine, may be unfamiliar in some contexts)

advancedVocab.vanno benissimo: are perfect;work very well (emphatic approval, not just 'are fine')

vocab.nella teglia: in the baking pan; in the tray

advancedVocab.stendere: to spread; to roll out (in cooking, refers to dough)
advancedVocab.mi ungo le mani: I get my hands greasy; I get my hands oily (note: 'ungere' means to grease/oil)
advancedVocab.una decina di minuti: about ten minutes; a dozen minutes (note: 'decina' means 'about ten')
advancedVocab.su per giù: more or less; roughly (idiomatic expression for approximation)

vocab.andiamo a: we go to; let's (do something) (used to introduce an action)

beginnerVocab.andiamo: we go; let's go (note: often used to mean 'let's' do something)

advancedVocab.tritare con le mani: crumble by hand; break up with hands (literally 'chop with hands', but here means to crumble or break apart manually)
advancedVocab.in questo caso qua: in this case here (emphatic, colloquial use of 'qua' for 'here')
advancedVocab.andiamo proprio a: we're really going to; we're actually going to (emphatic use of 'proprio' to stress the action)

vocab.stravolgere: to overturn; to mess up; to radically change
vocab.suona?: does it ring?; does it sound? (here: do you get it?)

advancedVocab.stravolgere: to completely change; to turn upside down (nuanced: to radically alter, not just modify)

advancedVocab.inforna: put in the oven (imperative form, from 'infornare')
advancedVocab.non te le dimenticare dentro: don't forget them inside (nuanced: 'them' refers to something previously mentioned, 'inside' means in the oven)
advancedVocab.suona?: does it ring a bell?; does it make sense? (colloquial, literally 'does it ring?')

vocab.mettiamo a cuocere: let's cook; we put to cook (note: 'mettiamo a cuocere' literally means 'we put to cook')

advancedVocab.mettiamo a cuocere: let's start cooking; let's put to cook (note: 'mettere a cuocere' is a set phrase for starting to cook something, not just 'put' or 'cook')

vocab.scamiciamo: we strip; we remove the shirt (from something)

advancedVocab.scamiciamo: we strip off the outer layer; we remove the 'shirt' (metaphor for peeling or removing an outer covering, often used for vegetables or objects)
advancedVocab.gli togliamo l'anima: we remove its core; we take out its soul (idiomatic, meaning to remove the innermost part, often used for food or objects)

vocab.Cosa fai: What are you doing; What do you do
vocab.gli togli: you remove from it; you take away from it

beginnerVocab.scamici: peel (note: refers to removing the skin, often of garlic)

advancedVocab.scamici: peel; remove the skin (from garlic; literally 'undress')
advancedVocab.frullandolo: blending it; pureeing it (with a blender)

advancedVocab.risponde troppo: answers too much; responds too much (nuanced: talks back, is too responsive)
advancedVocab.non posso essere cattivo: I can't be mean; I can't be bad (nuanced: I can't act harshly)
advancedVocab.cioè: that is; I mean (discourse marker, used to clarify or rephrase)

vocab.andremo a vedere: we will see; we are going to see

advancedVocab.filetto: filament; thin strip (here: the central sprout in garlic)
advancedVocab.anima: core; heart; soul (here: the central part of garlic)

vocab.nel pentolino: in the small pot; in the saucepan

vocab.per terra: on the ground; to the floor
vocab.ci andiamo sopra: we go on it; we step on it

advancedVocab.nell'ordine: in order; in tidiness (nuanced, refers to neatness or organization)
advancedVocab.butti per terra: throw on the ground (can imply carelessness or disregard)

vocab.sporchiamo: we get dirty; we make dirty
vocab.facciamo che: let's say that; let's do that
vocab.uno spicchio: a clove; a segment (note: usually of garlic or citrus)

vocab.anima: core; heart; soul (here: inner part of garlic)

advancedVocab.L'anima: the core; the soul (here: the inner part of garlic)

advancedVocab.andiamo a togliere: let's remove; let's take away (note: 'andiamo a' + infinitive is a common spoken way to suggest an action)
advancedVocab.non digerisco: I can't digest; I don't tolerate (note: can mean both literal and figurative intolerance in Italian)

advancedVocab.c'è dentro: there is inside; is part of (can imply involvement or presence within something)
advancedVocab.anima: soul; essence (can refer to the 'spirit' or 'core' of something, not just a literal soul)

vocab.andremo a: we will; we are going to

vocab.andiamo a: let's go to; we are going to

advancedVocab.sbriciolare: to crumble; to break into crumbs

advancedVocab.ciucciato: sucked (can mean to suck on something, often with a childish or playful nuance)

vocab.a coccolarlo: to cuddle it; to pamper it (refers to rice)
vocab.a guardarlo: to watch it; to look at it

beginnerVocab.coccolarlo: to cuddle it; to pamper it (note: 'lo' refers to 'riso' = rice)

advancedVocab.Non ti dico niente: I'm not telling you anything; I won't say anything (can imply annoyance or resignation)
advancedVocab.guarda: look; listen (used as a discourse marker to get attention)
advancedVocab.vado avanti io: I'll go ahead; I'll take over (nuanced: taking initiative or responsibility)
advancedVocab.coccolarlo il riso: pamper the rice; cuddle the rice (idiomatic: treat the rice with care, often used in cooking contexts)

vocab.faccio io: I'll do it; I'll handle it
vocab.ti tolgo: I'll remove for you; I'll take off for you
vocab.non ci serve: we don't need it; it's not needed (for us)

advancedVocab.faccio io: I'll do it; Let me handle it (emphatic, 'I'll take care of it')
advancedVocab.non ci serve: we don't need it; it's not needed (literally 'it doesn't serve us', idiomatic)

vocab.mi sta simpatico: I like him; he's nice to me (literally: he is nice to me)

advancedVocab.mi sta simpatico: I like him; he's likable to me (note: 'stare simpatico' is an idiomatic way to say someone is likable to you, not literally 'stands nice')

vocab.fai te: you do it; you handle it
vocab.che ho fatto: that I did; that I made
vocab.giù: down; down there (note: often refers to southern Italy)

advancedVocab.Fai te?: You do it?; Up to you? (colloquial, can mean 'Will you handle it?' or 'Is it your turn?')
advancedVocab.giù in Sicilia: down in Sicily (colloquial use of 'giù' to mean 'down south', culturally refers to southern Italy)

vocab.me ne sono innamorato: I fell in love with it (note: 'me ne sono innamorato' literally means 'I fell in love with it/of it')

advancedVocab.all'anciova: with anchovy (dialect; 'anciova' is Sicilian for anchovy)
advancedVocab.come dicono loro: as they say (emphasizes local or regional way of saying something)
advancedVocab.me ne sono innamorato: I fell in love with it (nuanced reflexive construction; 'ne' refers to 'of it')

advancedVocab.però: however; but (discourse marker, can express surprise or contrast)
advancedVocab.carino: nice; cute (nuanced, context-dependent: can mean 'pleasant', 'interesting', or 'cute')
advancedVocab.provare a: try to (construction: 'provare a' + infinitive means 'to try to do something')
advancedVocab.col: with the (contraction of 'con il', sometimes missed by learners)

vocab.nella mia testa: in my head; in my mind

advancedVocab.un po' particolare: a bit unusual; somewhat special (nuanced, not just 'particular')

vocab.nel piatto: in the plate; on the plate
vocab.va coperto: must be covered; should be covered (impersonal construction)

advancedVocab.riprodurre nel piatto: recreate on the plate; reproduce on the plate (contextual: to imitate a visual effect in food presentation)
advancedVocab.mollica: breadcrumbs; soft part of bread (not crust)

vocab.da: from; gives (contextual, as in 'gives an effect')
vocab.un pochettino: a little bit; just a bit

advancedVocab.appena si asciuga un pochettino: as soon as it dries a little bit (note: 'appena' = 'as soon as', 'un pochettino' = 'a tiny bit')

vocab.alla mantecatura: at the creaming stage; during creaming (cooking term: final mixing for creamy texture)
vocab.mantecare: to cream; to mix until creamy (cooking term)
vocab.il fuoco: the heat; the flame; the stove

advancedVocab.mantecare: to cream; to stir in butter/cheese (cooking term)

vocab.l'ho spento: I turned it off; I switched it off

advancedVocab.va avanti di cottura: keeps cooking; continues to cook (nuanced: food continues to cook even after heat is off)

advancedVocab.Mettiamo: Let's put; we put (first person plural, present indicative or imperative)
advancedVocab.girare: to stir; to turn (here, 'stir' in cooking context)

vocab.vai a: you go to; you are going to (expresses intention/future)

advancedVocab.incorporare: to incorporate; to blend in (culinary context)
advancedVocab.rompere l'amido: break the starch (culinary process; not literal breaking)
advancedVocab.quella cremosità: that creaminess (refers to a specific texture in Italian cooking)

advancedVocab.fai come vedi in tanti fare: do as you see many (people) do; imitate what you see many doing
advancedVocab.la famosa onda: the famous wave (refers to a well-known dance or movement, possibly culturally specific)

vocab.mi avete portato: you brought me; you have brought me
vocab.però: though; however; but (adds emphasis or contrast)

advancedVocab.super prepara: super prepared (note: likely a truncated or playful form of 'super preparato')
advancedVocab.non rompere la cucina: don't mess up the kitchen; don't break the kitchen (note: 'rompere' literally means 'to break', but here it means 'don't make a mess' or 'don't ruin it')

vocab.andiamo a: let's go to; we are going to

advancedVocab.si è sciolto: has melted; has dissolved (note: reflexive construction, often used for substances becoming liquid)
advancedVocab.andiamo a mettere: let's go add; we're going to add (note: idiomatic use of 'andare a' + infinitive to indicate immediate future or intention)
advancedVocab.di aceto o di limone: some vinegar or some lemon (note: 'di' used partitive, not 'of')

vocab.andremo a: we will (go to); we are going to
vocab.bello: nice; beautiful (here: 'nice', as in 'a nice plate')

advancedVocab.andremo a mettere: we are going to put (future intention; periphrastic future)
advancedVocab.bello: nice; attractive (can be used as an intensifier, not just 'beautiful')

advancedVocab.dandy: dandy (stylish, fashion-conscious man; sometimes with flamboyant or eccentric connotations)
advancedVocab.ditate: fingerprints; smudges (note: informal, literally 'finger marks', can refer to physical marks or metaphorically to personal touch/influence)

vocab.dal cliente: to the client; from the client
vocab.tutte le ditate: all the fingerprints; all the smudges
vocab.le botte: the hits; the knocks; the blows

advancedVocab.tò: here you go; take this (colloquial, used when handing something over)
advancedVocab.ditate: fingerprints; smudges (from 'dita' = fingers, refers to marks left by fingers)
advancedVocab.dare le botte sotto: to hit from below; to knock underneath (idiomatic, can mean to apply force or pressure from below, sometimes metaphorical)

vocab.si danno: they give (to each other); one gives (impersonal)

advancedVocab.danno le botte: they hit; they knock (colloquial for tapping or banging, often on a container or bowl)
advancedVocab.scuote: shake (from 'scuotere', can mean to shake or to agitate)
advancedVocab.incorporare aria: incorporate air (culinary term, to mix in air for fluffiness)

vocab.che c'è: that there is; which is there

advancedVocab.intanto: meanwhile; in the meantime (can also mean 'for now', context-dependent)
advancedVocab.lo vai a stendere: you're going to spread it out; you're going to lay it out (construction 'andare a + infinitive' emphasizes intention or imminence, not literal 'go')

vocab.andiamo a: let's go to; we are going to
vocab.come vogliamo: as we want; how we want

advancedVocab.un po': a bit; a little (can mean 'somewhat', not just quantity)

vocab.va bene così: is it okay like this; is this fine
vocab.gioco di colori: play of colors; color contrast (visual effect)

advancedVocab.va bene così: is it okay like this; is this fine (set phrase, can mean 'is this enough')
advancedVocab.gioco di colori: play of colors; color contrast (nuanced expression, refers to visual effect)

vocab.andiamo a: let's go to; we go to

advancedVocab.andiamo a mettere: let's go put; let's add (colloquial, often used in cooking instructions)

vocab.andiamo a: let's go to; we are going to

advancedVocab.sbriciolata: crumble; sprinkling (culinary term, breaking into crumbs)

advancedVocab.l'effetto che deve fare in bocca: the effect it should have in the mouth (nuanced: refers to taste/texture experience, not literal effect)

vocab.sei stato bravo: you did well; you were good

advancedVocab.alla mollica: with breadcrumbs (regional pasta preparation; literally 'with crumb')

vocab.devi essere: you must be; you have to be
